name: Cookstove Demonstration
dates: Sat Mar 7 11AM-3PM; Sat Mar 21 11AM-3PM; Sat Apr 4 11AM-3PM; Sat Apr 18 11AM-3PM; Sun Apr 26 11AM-3PM; Sat May 2 11AM-3PM; Sat May 16 11AM-3PM; Sun May 31 11AM-3PM
note: After a walk through the Longstreet Farmhouse, stop by the outdoor kitchen to see what's cooking on the wood-fired stove. Discover how recipes, cooking techniques and kitchens have changed since the 1890s.

name: Accordion Melodies of the 1890s
dates: Sat Mar 21 1-3PM; Sat Apr 18 1-3PM; Sat May 16 1-3PM
note: Enjoy melodies of the 1890s played on the accordion while you visit the Longstreet farmhouse.

name: Piano Ballads from the Turn of the Century
dates: Sat Mar 28 1-3PM; Sat Apr 11 1-3PM; Sat May 30 1-3PM
note: Tap your feet, dance a one-step, or sing along to popular turn-of-the-19th-century ballads as they are played on the farmhouse piano.

name: Blacksmith Demonstration
dates: Sun Mar 8 1-3PM; Sun Apr 12 1-3PM; Sun May 10 1-3PM
note: The blacksmith will be working at the anvil turning iron into functional tools and hardware. See craftsmanship in action and learn about the rich history behind the forge.

name: 19th Century Woodworking Demonstration
note: Woodworking is more than carpentry. See how a woodworker studies his materials, plies his tools, and crafts beautiful and functional items.

name: Wood Carving Demonstration
note: Using a collection of knives, gauges, and their imagination, see how our wood carver whittles a block of wood into a beautiful work of art.

name: Traditional Quilting Demonstration
note: Traditional hand quilting is becoming a lost art. Be inspired as our quilter demonstrates beautiful quilting stitches so you can create your own heirloom quilt.

name: Rug Hooking Demonstration
note: A demonstration of the 19th-century craft of rug hooking.

name: Felting Demonstration
note: Felted wool is the world's oldest known textile. Learn the process of felting wool that can be used in garments.

name: Embroidery Demonstration
note: Embroidery is the skilled art of applying decorative designs with a needle and thread. Be inspired by stitches and embellishments to create a beautiful piece of clothing or decoration.

name: Traditional Chair Caning Demonstration
note: Chair caning is an intricate skill. See the process of weaving natural plant fibers into the seat of a chair, creating a delicate but sturdy design.

name: The Libby Prison Minstrels
note: Visit the farm and be entertained by the Libby Prison Minstrels, known for their historical songs about life on the battlefield and the home front during the Civil War era.

name: Wool Days / Sheep Shearing
note: Learn how farmers use traditional blade shearing practices to remove the fleece from sheep. Gene Sheninger will return with his collies to demonstrate herding skills.

name: Decoration Day Celebration
note: Before it was called Memorial Day, Americans celebrated Decoration Day. This family holiday originated after the Civil War for family reunions and remembrance of fallen soldiers.

name: Morning Farm Chores
dates: Sat Mar 7; Sun Mar 22; Sat Apr 4; Sun Apr 19; Sat May 2; Sun May 17 (all 8-9:30AM)
note: Help the farm staff with morning chores before the farm opens. Experience milking a cow, collecting eggs, and feeding the livestock. Ages 6+; under 18 must be with adult. Closed-toe shoes required. ONE session per season per participant.

name: Adult Farm Chores
note: Adults-only farm chores: milk a cow, collect eggs, feed livestock, with detailed discussions about 1890s farm life. Closed-toe shoes and clothes that can get dirty required.

name: Beginner Wood Carving Class
note: Get a foundation in traditional wood carving. Complete two projects; carving supplies and two extra pieces of wood are yours to take home. All supplies provided by instructor.

name: Rug Hooking Gathering
note: Gather with other rug hookers or learn techniques from experienced rug hookers. Bring an existing project or come for inspiration. Bring your lunch.

name: Farm Buds
note: Growing a garden! Each week, learn about a new plant, spend time in the garden, visit the chickens, and head to the farmhouse for stories and games. Plant seeds to take home.

name: Walnford Day
location: Historic Walnford, 62 Walnford Rd, Upper Freehold
note: Annual celebration of 200 years of Waln history! Historical demonstrations, gristmill in action, horse-drawn wagon ride, 1773 Waln family home tour, and bluegrass music by Magnolia Street String Band 2-4PM.

name: Milling Demonstrations
note: Experience sights and sounds of years past as corn is ground into meal at Waln's Mill. Demonstrations on weekend afternoons April–November.

name: Spring Eggstravaganza
note: Egg hunt where children search for eggs filled with treats hidden throughout the site, followed by an egg and spoon race and a photo with a chicken! Starts promptly at 1PM.

name: Fine Feathered Friends
note: Learn what Walnford's chickens eat, where they live, and how many eggs they lay. Interact with the flock and make a take-home craft.

name: Natural Dyeing Demonstration
note: In the 1700s, all dyes came from natural sources. Explore the magic of color at the Holmes-Hendrickson House. Kelly Bahash demonstrates locally grown plants used to make natural dyes.

name: Spinning, Knitting & Weaving Demonstration
note: Enjoy a demonstration of spinning, knitting, and weaving at the Holmes-Hendrickson House (c. 1754), an example of New Jersey's colonial past.

name: Historic Seabrook-Wilson House Tours
note: Visit the Seabrook-Wilson House, dating back to the early 1700s — one of the oldest homes along the Jersey shore and the oldest along Sandy Hook Bay.

name: Historic Portland Place Tours
note: Owned by the Hartshorne family for most of its history, Portland Place is interwoven with the early settlement of the Navesink Highlands. Tours limited to 10 people.

name: Historic Racing Stable Tours
note: Located within the Brookdale Farm National Historic District, the racing stable showcases the history of Thoroughbred racing in Monmouth County through exhibits in the horse stalls.

name: Historic Battery Lewis Tours
location: Hartshorne Woods Park, Rocky Point, Highlands
note: The restored Battery Lewis at the 224-acre Navesink Military Reservation includes interpretive exhibits and a 16-inch gun barrel.

name: Friday Evening Pottery
price: $140/person (7 sessions, includes glazes, firings, 12 lbs clay)
note: Discover throwing and hand building with clay. Great family class. Participants 13 and under must register with an adult. No makeup classes.

name: Friday Evening Pottery
price: $140/person (7 sessions, includes glazes, firings, 12 lbs clay)
note: Discover throwing and hand building with clay. Great family class. Participants 13 and under must register with an adult. No makeup classes.

name: Puppet Making / Puppet Show Day!
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Tulip Room
note: World Puppetry Day! Make paper plate puppets then put on a puppet show. All supplies provided.

name: Sunday Ceramics
dates: Sun Mar 1; Sun Apr 12; Sun May 3
note: Choose from bisque fired pottery pieces to glaze in your own style. Piece is glaze fired and ready to pick up in about a week. Space is limited.

name: Flashlight Easter Eggstravaganza
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Gym A
note: Grab your flashlight! Easter Bunny is supervising an egg clean-up. Help collect eggs, create spring crafts, and enjoy a visit with the Bunny.

name: Mum! Dad! Bingo! Bluey™!
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Gym A
note: Step into the world of Bluey™! Musical Statues, Pass the Parcel, Keepy Uppy, Raiders, plus a Bluey™-themed craft and scavenger hunt.

name: Agatha Christie's 'The Mousetrap'
note: A snowstorm traps strangers in a remote manor; a murder has been committed. The world's longest running play. Pre-registration required.

name: Under the Spring Stars
location: Dorbrook Recreation Area Soccer Field Parking Lot
note: Gaze into the night skies searching for constellations, planets, satellites, and shooting stars. Bring a camp chair or blanket and a flashlight. Cancelled if overcast.

name: Animal Hours at a Park Near You!
note: Get a close-up view of animals from the Huber Woods Reptile House. What does a living snake feel like? Is it a toad or a frog?

name: Family Nature Days!
location: Seven Presidents Oceanfront Park Activity Center
note: A day filled with nature: animal presentation, guided walk, and craft suited for children of all ages.

name: Family Woodcock Walk
location: Dorbrook Recreation Area (Parking Lot Southside of Rt. 537)
note: Evening walk to search for the American Woodcock's spectacular sky dance mating ritual. Waterproof shoes and flashlight recommended.

name: Dark Sky Hike
location: Huber Woods Park Environmental Center Courtyard
note: Celebrate International Dark Sky Week with a guided night hike searching for nocturnal wonders. Learn how outdoor lighting affects wildlife. Closed-toe shoes required.

name: Eagle Boat Tours
note: America 250-themed evening pontoon tour on Manasquan Reservoir searching for bald eagles. Wear red, white, and blue! Life jackets provided and required.

name: Forest Friends After Five
location: Manasquan Reservoir Environmental Center Parking Lot
note: Gentle nature-filled stroll for young children and families. Look for bugs, listen to birds, enjoy sensory activities. Perfect before bedtime.

name: Spring Peeper Stroll
location: Freneau Woods Park Visitor Center Parking Area
note: Hike quietly to wooded pools to catch a glimpse of spring peeper tree frogs — a sure sign spring has arrived!

name: Sunset Stroll Around Turkey Swamp Lake
note: Naturalist-guided walk around Turkey Swamp Lake just before sunset, finishing next to a crackling bonfire. Bring s'mores supplies and a flashlight.

name: World Turtle Day Celebration
location: Manasquan River Greenway Winter Run Activity Center
note: Live turtle show to celebrate World Turtle Day. Meet native turtle species and learn about their threats and how to help them.

name: Earth Day Open House
note: Celebrate Earth Day with live animal shows, guided nature and history walks. Bring a gently used nature book to exchange for a new one.

name: Earth Day Beach Cleanup
note: Help clean our coastline of trash and plastic debris. Bring your own gloves; trash bags and tools supplied. Meet near the fishing pier.

name: Earth Day Planting
note: Help restore our native maritime forest by planting native plants. First come, first served — plants in limited supply.

name: Seashore Open House
location: Seven Presidents Oceanfront Park Activity Center
note: Hands-on seashore activities at Seven Presidents Oceanfront Park. Speak with a Naturalist about your shell collection or aquatic animals on display. Call 732-872-2670.

name: Rockin' the Trails (Scavenger Hunt)
note: America 250th birthday cross-county scavenger hunt! Use the brochure to find hidden trail markers with patriotic symbols. Return brochure for grand prize entry. Call 732-872-2670.

name: Weekend Boat Tours at Manasquan Reservoir
location: Manasquan Reservoir Visitor Center, Windeler Road
note: 45-minute narrated boat tours with chances to view turtles, egrets, herons, ospreys, and possibly a bald eagle. Life jackets provided and required. Call 732-751-9453.

name: Kayak Fishing the Manasquan Reservoir
location: Manasquan Reservoir Environmental Center Outdoor Area
note: Fish for tiger muskies, largemouth bass, channel catfish, perch, crappie, and bluegill from sit-on-top kayaks. Bring gear or borrow ours.

name: Freshwater Fishing Derbies
dates: Sat Jun 20 2-5PM (Manasquan Res); Sun Jun 21 2-5PM (Turkey Swamp)
note: Annual fishing derbies for beginners and experienced anglers. Free casting/rigging clinic 12:30-1:30PM. Prizes for longest fish. Worms included. Fishing poles rent $5.

name: Canoe Rentals on Marlu Lake
note: Rent a canoe at Thompson Park's Marlu Lake. All equipment provided. Must return by 3PM. Available every other weekend from Memorial Day through August.

name: Climb Time
location: Shark River Park (Apr 10) / Swimming River Park (May 8)
note: Experience the thrill of climbing our 25-foot portable climbing wall. Must be 42 inches or taller to climb.

name: Open Shoot Archery
dates: Sat Mar 14; Sat Apr 11; Sat May 16 (all 10AM-1PM)
note: Improve your archery skills at Thompson Park Activity Barn's indoor range. NOT for beginners — take an instructional class first. Dress for weather (barn not heated/cooled).

name: Archery Golf
note: Learn the basics of archery, then shoot a round of archery golf on our pop-up course using soft-tipped arrows. Score a birdie instead of a bullseye!

name: Finding the Founding Fathers (GPS Hunt)
note: America's 250th anniversary GPS scavenger hunt! Use hand-held GPS units around the park to locate hiding spots of the founding fathers.

name: Spring into Spring Family Camping
location: Turkey Swamp Park Nomoco Group Campground
note: Overnight family camping with camping skills instruction, games, portable rock wall, and more. Platform tent, dinner, and breakfast provided.

name: ART Capades
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: All about the process! Each week a new project, medium, and materials. Splatter painting, playdough, stamped art, mixing colors, ornaments, and more.

name: ART Capades
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: All about the process! Each week a new project, medium, and materials. Splatter painting, playdough, stamped art, mixing colors, ornaments, and more.

name: ART Capades
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: All about the process! Each week a new project, medium, and materials. Splatter painting, playdough, stamped art, mixing colors, ornaments, and more.

name: Craft 'n' Play
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: Crafts exploring colors, textures, paint, glitter, and glue while exploring seasons and holidays. Plus time in the play area.

name: Craft 'n' Play
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: Crafts exploring colors, textures, paint, glitter, and glue while exploring seasons and holidays. Plus time in the play area.

name: Craft 'n' Play
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: Crafts exploring colors, textures, paint, glitter, and glue while exploring seasons and holidays. Plus time in the play area.

name: Messy Art
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: Toddlers delight in paint, glue, glitter, and more. Moves outdoors during nice weather.

name: Messy Art
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: Toddlers delight in paint, glue, glitter, and more. Moves outdoors during nice weather.

name: Messy Art
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Program Room B
note: Toddlers delight in paint, glue, glitter, and more. Moves outdoors during nice weather.

name: Let's Create!
note: Circle time for socialization and fine motor skills, featuring a story and an art project to bring home. No additional family members please.

name: Let's Create!
note: Circle time for socialization and fine motor skills, featuring a story and an art project to bring home. No additional family members please.

name: KidzArt Squiggles to Grins
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Tulip Room / Henry Hudson Trail Activity Center
dates: Multiple series: Tue Mar 31–May 5; Tue May 12–Jun 16 (Thompson); Thu Apr 2–May 7; Thu May 14–Jun 18 (Henry Hudson)
note: Fun and creative activities to enhance motor skills and develop language and social skills using fun and safe materials.

name: Mommy and Me for Mother's Day
note: Mothers and children paint together recreating a floral painting by Renoir!

name: Dr. Seuss Story Week
dates: Multiple sessions Mar 4-12 (Horton Hears a Who, Cat in the Hat, The Lorax, Hop on Pop, Fox in Socks)
note: Celebrate Dr. Seuss's birthday! Each session features a different beloved book with a story, craft, and games. Dress for mess or bring a smock.

name: Afternoon Tea: Mother's Day Edition
note: Make Mother's Day treats with Mrs. Chef: Pinwheel Tea Sandwiches, Heart Shaped 'Pop Tarts', plus one more treat. Finish with an old-fashioned Mother's Day Card.

name: Fun with Minions (Cake Decorating)
note: Get creative with cake and frosting with Mrs. Chef! Focus on design using a chocolate box mix. You'll be amazed at what you can do with store-bought ingredients!

name: Kids Springtime Cupcakes
note: Create springtime cupcakes with Mrs. Chef! Learn to follow directions, use food coloring, then assemble and decorate.

name: Parent/Child Food Truck Favorites
note: Make food truck faves! Menu: Grilled Pizza on a Stick, Frito Pie, and Ice Cream Sodas or M&M Ice Cream Cookie Sandwiches.

name: Red, White, and Blue Tea Party
note: A patriotic themed tea party — make a snack, drink tea, do a craft, and enjoy quality time together.

name: Shamrock Cut-Up Cake
note: Make a St. Patrick's Day Shamrock cut-up cake! Single-layer cakes cut into shapes, rearranged, and iced.

name: Spring Bunny Cut-Up Cake
note: Make a spring bunny cut-up cake using chocolate cake mix with food coloring design techniques.

name: Summer Treats
note: Create fun summertime treats with Mrs. Chef as you gear up for summer holidays. Menu TBA.

name: Creature Comforts at Your Fingertips
note: Mindful parent-child workshop: explore simple ways to find calm using breath, awareness, and hands. MELT exercises, breathwork, and mindfulness activities. Includes a copy of the book.

name: Keyboard Beginnings Level 1
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Tulip Room
note: Young children learn the names of musical keys and play short songs on keyboards. Recommended to have keyboard at home. Includes music book, rhythm instrument, crayons, and lesson outlines.

name: Keyboard Beginnings Level 2
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Tulip Room
note: Continuation of Level 1. Play new songs, read notes on the musical staff. Includes Keyboard Beginnings Music Book 2, lesson outlines, flash cards, and concentration game.

name: Little Learners
note: Introduction to literacy using play, movement, art, stories, and circle time. Much of the session outdoors when weather allows.

name: Little Learners
note: Introduction to literacy using play, movement, art, stories, and circle time. Much of the session outdoors when weather allows.

name: Morning Tea Party
note: Themed stories, snacks, and making a simple snack and tea together. Make new friends!

name: You, Me & Karaoke
location: Tatum Park Red Hill Activity Center
note: Family-friendly karaoke and dance party! Song requests, group sing-alongs, holiday classics. Dancing encouraged! Bring your own snacks and non-alcoholic drinks.

name: Mommy and Me Painted Flower Pots
note: Paint a flower pot together and plant seeds to watch flowers grow at home. For mothers and all mother figures to celebrate Mother's Day.

name: Storytime in the Garden: Growing Vegetable Soup
note: Springtime story about growing vegetables, followed by a spring-themed craft and a garden walk to see what's growing.

name: Storytime in the Garden: Planting a Rainbow
dates: Fri Mar 13; Fri Mar 20; Sat Apr 18 (10-11AM)
note: Story time about planting rainbow seeds and watching a garden grow, followed by a rainbow-themed craft.

name: Abracadabra, It's Spring!
note: Story time about spring magic, followed by a magical craft and a walk around the pond.

name: Case File: Whose Toes are Those?
note: Become a nature investigator! Identify animals by their toes and feet to learn about habitat, diet, and activity. Finish with animal tracks bingo.

name: Avian Anatomy 101
location: Manasquan River Greenway Winter Run Activity Center
note: What do we have in common with birds? Learn about bird anatomy, make a take-home bird craft, and take a nature walk. Your kiddos will be 'raven' about it!

name: Curious Kids
note: Encourage your child's natural curiosity with nature artifacts, crafts, stories, and nature walks. New topic each week. Dress for the weather.

name: Forest Bathing with Kids
location: Freneau Woods Park Visitor Center Parking Area
note: De-stress with a forest-bathing adventure. Mindful hour in the woods listening to trees and birds. Closed-toe shoes required.

name: Grow with Love
note: Mother's Day nature adventure: story time, plant a seed to take home, then a gentle walk to explore flowers, trees, and tiny creatures.

name: Growing Up Wild
location: Multiple Parks (Huber Woods, Thompson Park, Holmdel Park)
note: Preschool series exploring a different park each week — guided walks and nature activities. Different park each session.

name: How to Catch a Garden Fairy
note: Help Naturalists catch a garden fairy! Create a fairy-themed craft, listen to a story, and go on a garden walk. Indoor and outdoor components.

name: Hue Hike
location: Deep Cut Gardens / Freneau Woods Park
note: Walk where children explore nature through the lens of a single color. Toddler's pace, building observation skills and sparking curiosity.

name: My First Fish
note: A Naturalist helps you and your child catch their first fish! Catch-and-release. Bait, poles, and instruction provided.

name: Nature Tots
dates: Chipmunk Series: Fri Mar 6-20; Purple Martin Series: Wed Apr 1-15; Butterfly Series: Thu May 7-21
note: Outdoor fun with toddlers on scenic park trails with nature discovery and craft activities. Designed for short legs and short attention spans. Children under 2 welcome free.

name: Springing Into Sunshine
note: Preschool series exploring nature topics with short walks, games, and crafts. At times part of the program takes place indoors.

name: Hiking Adventures
dates: Sat Mar 21 (Thompson Pk); Sat Apr 18 (Big Brook Pk); Fri May 22 (Deep Cut Gardens)
note: Short hikes designed for little legs and short attention spans. Children under 2 welcome free. Bring a back or front carrier.

name: Let's Explore
note: Sensory play, fine and large motor activities, simple art projects, story time, and nature exploration. Dress for mess. No class 3/30 & 4/6.

name: Let's Explore
note: Sensory play, fine and large motor activities, simple art projects, story time, and nature exploration. Dress for mess. No class 5/25.

name: Miss Melissa's Aardvarks
location: Dorbrook Recreation Area / Tatum Park / Freneau Woods
note: Music for Aardvarks: early childhood music development with bells, drums, shakers, sticks, and original music from Jack's Big Music Show. Teacher plays guitar; children sing and dance.

name: Mommy & Me Ballet
location: Tatum Park Red Hill Activity Center
dates: Tue Mar 24–Jun 9; Wed Mar 25–Jun 10 (9:30-10:15AM, no class 4/7-8 & 5/12-13)
note: Pre-ballet class! Basic ballet movement through props and fun songs. Adults dressed to move. Students wear ballet shoes and attire.

name: Morning Playtime
location: Dorbrook / Freneau Woods / Tatum Park / Thompson Park
dates: Multiple series starting Mar 12 and Apr 23 at various locations
note: Build creativity and confidence through exercise, music, and specialized play equipment. Tunnels, mini-trampoline, ribbon sticks, and bells. Parent must be present; no drop-offs.

name: Move and Groove
note: Introduction to dance and movement! Sing, dance, and explore while building basic motor skills using scarves, beanbags, and musical instruments.

name: My Morning Playtime
dates: Multiple series: Thu Mar 12–Apr 9; Thu Apr 23–May 21; Wed Apr 1-29; Wed May 6–Jun 3
note: Most active Playtime class for preschoolers ready for a little independence. Free-play with tunnels and trampolines, then themed and movement activities.

name: PreK Prep
note: Get your child ready for school: number recognition, counting, letter recognition, seasons, days of week, shapes, and free play.

name: Sensory Explorers
note: Explore the world through sound, vision, and touch! Multi-sensory activities, messy art, song, and dance. Designed to grow young children's brains and fine motor skills.

name: Superheroes
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Gym A
dates: Avengers: Mon Mar 2-30; Justice League: Mon Apr 13–May 11
note: Unleash your child's inner superhero! After a story, soar, climb, jump, run, and have fun. Different superhero each week. Avengers series (Mar) and Justice League series (Apr-May).

name: The Power of Play
location: Fort Monmouth Recreation Center Gym A
note: Structured play using games and recreation to reinforce following directions, taking turns, motor skills, and social play. Fun intro to competition for young children.

name: When You Wish Upon a Star
location: Tatum Park Holland Activity Center / Freneau Woods Park
dates: Multiple series and locations starting Mar 11
note: Walt Disney® themed program with classic stories, crafts, fun with food, and exciting activities. Different Disney theme each week.

name: When You Wish Upon a Star-Spangled Banner
location: Freneau Woods Park / Tatum Park Holland Activity Center
dates: Multiple series and locations starting Mar 11
note: America 250th Disney program! Red, white, and blue crafts, classic stories, fun with food, and movement. Mickey's early days to modern heroes like Elsa and Moana.

name: Basketball with Me & Dad/Mom
dates: Multiple series: Mon Apr 6–27; Sat Apr 11–May 2; Mon May 11–Jun 8; Sat May 16–Jun 20
note: Parent and child practice passing, shooting, and dribbling together. Parent participation required.

name: Hat Trick Hockey - Mites
dates: Thu Apr 9-30; Sat Apr 11–May 2; Thu May 14–Jun 4
note: Fun start in sports with sneakers (not skates) and soft equipment! Parental assistance required during each class. Bring a camera!

name: Summer Job Fair
note: Explore Park System summer job opportunities: Camp Counselors, Lifeguards, Boat Tour Operators, Rangers, Greenskeepers, and more!

name: E. Murray Todd Half Marathon
location: Brookdale Community College Collins Arena, Lincroft
note: Over 45 years of racing tradition! 13.1 miles on one of New Jersey's most attractive courses through Lincroft, Holmdel, and Colts Neck.

name: Spring Craft Show
note: Support local artists and artisans. Shop for Mother's Day, Father's Day, graduations, weddings, and more!

name: Creative Arts Festival
note: Family-friendly festival with fine artists and artisans, plus live music by top local musicians.

name: Native Plant Workshop & Exchange
note: Free native plant lecture followed by a native plant exchange. Swap native plants from your garden with others.

name: Splendid Spring Strolls
dates: Wed Mar 11; Wed Mar 18; Wed Apr 15; Wed Apr 29; Wed May 13; Wed May 21
note: Join a Park System Naturalist on a peaceful trail walk. Trees blossoming, birds returning from migration, and tons of topics to discuss!

name: Casual Birder
dates: Tue Mar 24; Tue Apr 28; Tue May 5; Tue May 19
note: FREE laid-back morning bird walks with a Park System Naturalist for about an hour and a half. No expertise needed.

name: Menhaden: The Most Important Fish Along the Jersey Shore!
note: Learn about the menhaden — a keystone species critical to the Jersey Shore ecosystem.

name: Horseshoe Crabs Trying to Survive in a Modern World
note: Learn about horseshoe crabs, an ancient creature that comes ashore each spring to mate and lay eggs as it has for 400+ million years.

name: Kids Kixx Pee Wee
location: Dorbrook Rec Area / Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr / Holmdel Park
note: Fun soccer basics through games, demonstrations, and modified drills. Small classes with walk-along parent assistance required.

name: Kixx Soccer & Crafts
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Gym A
note: Combines soccer skills with art projects. Builds gross and fine motor skills, socialization, and preschool readiness.

name: Kixx Soccer & Crafts: World Cup Edition!
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Outdoor Area
note: Soccer + crafts with a World Cup theme — exploring countries, flags, and fun cultural elements alongside skill-building.

name: Mini Pee Wee Sports
note: Intro to movement: throwing, kicking, running, jumping. Fun noncompetitive setting designed for toddlers with parent guidance.

name: Pee Wee Sports
note: Fundamentals of sport through fun, skill-building games. Coordination, teamwork, and confidence in a positive noncompetitive environment.

name: Mini-Hawk Multi Sport
note: Multi-sport program: flag football, lacrosse, and track & field. Safe, structured environment with encouragement and fun.

name: T-Ball with Me & Dad/Mom
note: Learn catching, throwing, and hitting off the tee. Noncompetitive with parent participation required.

name: Build a Flower Pot
note: Hand-build a clay flower pot. Learn basic techniques and embellish with glazes. Ready for pickup 2 weeks after.

name: Ceramic Bunnies
note: Glaze and decorate a pre-cast ceramic Easter bunny. Kiln-fired and ready for pickup one week after class.

name: Children's Ceramics
note: Decorate pre-cast ceramic items. Learn about greenware, bisque, underglaze, and glaze. Fee includes instruction, tools, firings.

name: Children's Ceramics
note: Decorate pre-cast ceramic items. Learn about greenware, bisque, underglaze, and glaze. Fee includes instruction, tools, firings.

name: Children's Handbuilding with Clay
note: Create functional or decorative clay works using slab and coil construction. Includes 10 lbs clay, glazes, and firings.

name: Children's Handbuilding with Clay
note: Create functional or decorative clay works using slab and coil construction. Includes 10 lbs clay, glazes, and firings.

name: Children's Wheel-Thrown Pottery
note: Learn wheel-throwing and hand-building with instructor demos and individual guidance. Includes tools, 10 lbs clay, glazes, firings.

name: Children's Wheel-Thrown Pottery
note: Learn wheel-throwing and hand-building with instructor demos and individual guidance. Includes tools, 10 lbs clay, glazes, firings.

name: Children's Wheel-Thrown Pottery
note: Learn wheel-throwing and hand-building with instructor demos and individual guidance. Includes tools, 10 lbs clay, glazes, firings.

name: Children's Wheel-Thrown Pottery
note: Learn wheel-throwing and hand-building with instructor demos and individual guidance. Includes tools, 10 lbs clay, glazes, firings.

name: Mother's Day Plate
note: Paint and decorate a ceramic plate as a Mother's Day gift. Kiln-fired, ready for pickup in one week.

name: Springtime Birdhouses
note: Build and paint a clay birdhouse. Hand-building skills included. Ready for pickup two weeks after class.

name: Aspiring Artists for Homeschoolers
note: Paint famous masterpieces as inspiration using mixed media. Each series ends with an original seasonal design. Instructor: Debra Stasiak.

name: Great Impressionism for 5, 6 & 7 Year Olds
dates: Mar 7–28, Mar 4–25, Apr 1–22, Apr 4–25, Apr 29–May 20, May 2–30
note: Learn Impressionism/Fauvism brushstrokes using acrylic paints. Paint famous masterpieces plus original compositions. Instructor: Debra Stasiak.

name: Great Impressionism for Aspiring Young Artists
note: Paint famous Impressionist masterpieces on canvas board. Includes original compositions. Instructor: Debra Stasiak.

name: Creating Iconic Comic Book Characters
note: Learn to draw iconic comic book characters with publisher Derek 'Lonzo Starr' Allen. Combines drawing and creative writing.

name: KidzArt Discover
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center / Henry Hudson Trail Activity Center
note: Art as a new language: drawing skills, confidence, focus, social interaction, and Kindergarten readiness. KidzArt curriculum.

name: KidzArt Explore
location: Henry Hudson Trail Act Ctr / Fort Monmouth / Thompson Park
note: Creative thinking through drawing using charcoal, watercolor, chalk pastel, oil pastel, and marker. Complete a piece each class.

name: KidzArt Painting Workshop
location: Henry Hudson Trail Act Ctr / Thompson Park Creative Arts Center
dates: Mar 14, Apr 18, May 16 (Sweet Treats / Spring Bunny / Spring Flowers)
note: Step-by-step painting instruction with encouragement for personal creativity. All supplies included.

name: TeenzArt Studio
location: Henry Hudson Trail Act Ctr / Fort Monmouth / Thompson Park
note: Drawing, sculpture, watercolor, pastels, painting, and mixed media. Creative freedom in a relaxed, fun atmosphere.

name: American Girl Doll Design
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Program Room A
note: Create fun accessories for 18" dolls using hot glue gun under supervision. Child must bring their own doll.

name: Books & Cooks
location: Dorbrook Rec Area Visitor Center / Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr
note: Explore basic recipes, practice fine motor skills, and read stories while food cooks. Kitchen safety basics in a fun environment.

name: Cooking Basics for Teens
note: Hands-on cooking: chopping, measuring, reading recipes. Fun and tasty recipes you can recreate at home. No experience needed.

name: Dinner Party Chefs
note: Prepare a full dinner party menu: hors d'oeuvres, salad, entrée, side, and dessert. Learn proper technique and kitchen safety.

name: Kids Baking
note: Hands-on baking: different treats each week. Proper measuring, mixing, following a recipe, and more.

name: Let's Cook
note: Fun and creative foods with basic culinary skills: reading recipes, chopping, measuring, kitchen safety. At least one recipe per class.

name: Let's Decorate Cupcakes for America's 250th Birthday!
note: Decorate patriotic cupcakes with buttercream icing. Learn stars, shells, flowers, and more. Take home your beautifully decorated cupcakes.

name: Tween Chefs
note: Expand palates with simple meals, culinary basics, and meal planning. Limited class sizes. Bring an apron.

name: Wee Can Cook
note: Chop, roll, measure, spread, mix, toss, and pour! Yummy hands-on cooking projects with food-related stories. Bring an apron.

name: Young Chefs
note: Simple entrées each week. Kitchen safety and healthy eating habits discussed. Limited class sizes. Bring an apron.

name: Among Us at Fort Monmouth
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Gym A
note: Real-life version of the popular social deduction game with multiple rounds and Among Us activities.

name: Beginner Chess
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Beech Room
note: Learn piece names, movements, and how to win, lose, or draw. Each session: instruction then play. Chess boards/pieces provided.

name: Child and Babysitting Safety
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Beech Room
note: Safety and injury prevention, conflict resolution, age-appropriate play, and basic first aid. Builds confidence for caring for children.

name: Junior Detective Workshop
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Beech Room
note: Fingerprinting, grid searches, invisible ink, UV puzzles, and CSI crime scenes. Taught by active duty and retired detectives.

name: Learn to Play Dungeons & Dragons
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Atrium Room
note: Beginner-friendly tabletop RPG. Create heroes, solve problems, and go on quests using imagination, dice, and teamwork. Dice included.

name: Minecraft at Fort Monmouth
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Gym A
note: Craft a pixelated pickaxe, go on a scavenger hunt for 'ores', make Creeper masks, and build blocky structures.

name: Snapology RoboPets Robotics & Coding
note: Build animal robot models (fish, alligator, dogs, frogs) that teach robotics design and coding fundamentals.

name: Snapology Mechanical Masterminds Engineering
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Program Room D
note: Explore mechanical movement through simple machines, physics, and engineering design using LEGO bricks.

name: Snapology Space Science
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Beech Room
note: Study the solar system via STEM/STEAM projects, LEGO builds, and robotic space stations. Learn about NASA, astronauts, and the universe.

name: Snapology Planes, Trains, and Automobiles
note: Explore transportation engineering: build trains, cars, and airplanes with LEGO kits. Learn energy, wheels, axles, and STEM concepts.

name: Snapology Movie Fan Favorites
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Program Room D
note: Build LEGO projects inspired by Toy Story, Harry Potter, Frozen, Star Wars, and more. Creative building with new friends.

name: Snapology Mega Machines Robotics & Coding
location: Thompson Park Visitor Center Beech Room
note: Build motorized vehicles and learn gear ratio, sensors, pulleys, cranks, and programming. Trucks, rovers, helicopters, and more.

name: Plentiful Ponds - A Freshwater Eco Study
location: Manasquan Reservoir Environmental Center Outdoor Area
note: Strap on waders and scoop up freshwater macroinvertebrates. Examine and identify fascinating species up close. All equipment provided.

name: Hooked on Fishing Birthday Parties
note: Party-goers learn to bait, cast, and fish for bluegills and largemouth bass. 1 hour fishing + 30 min party time in Elsa's Lodge.

name: Building Confidence Through Bike Riding
note: Lose the training wheels! Small class with individual attention. Parents assist the instructor and practice between sessions.

name: Take a Shot: Intro to Archery
note: Learn basics of archery for recreation, competition, or hunting. All equipment provided. Dress for the weather (barn not climate-controlled).

name: Take a Shot: Archery Series
note: Three-session archery series with new games each session. Beginner or refresher. All equipment provided.

name: Super Archers Academy
note: Superhero-themed archery session. Learn the same skills as your favorite hero. All equipment provided.

name: Young Explorers
location: Holmdel Park / Tatum Park / Hartshorne Woods Park
note: Hit the trails, learn trail etiquette and safety, spot wildlife, and make new friends. Pack water, snack, and dress for adventure.

name: Beginner Acting: The Magical Meezie Pirates at the USA's 250th Birthday Bash!
location: Freneau Woods Park / Manasquan River Greenway
note: Original play performed on the last day for family and friends. Includes acting games and performance skills.

name: Downtown Daily News Special Edition: USA's 250th Birthday Bash!
location: Big Brook Park Boundary Rd Activity Center / Freneau Woods Park
note: Original play with performance for family on last day. Covers vocal, movement, and creative acting skills.

name: Homeschool Troupe of Actors
note: Create monologues/scenes/play performed for family on last day. Voice, movement, imagination, and the Four Centers of Acting.

name: Good Morning Puppet Show!
note: Make your own puppet, play acting games, and perform stories, songs, and jokes for family on the last day.

name: The Afternoon Puppet Show
location: Big Brook Park / Freneau Woods Park
note: Make a puppet, play acting games, and perform stories, songs, and jokes for family on the last day.

name: Hip Hop Dance
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Group Fitness Room
dates: Mar 3–Apr 7 Tue, Apr 21–May 26 Tue (4:30 or 5:30 PM)
note: Breaking, crumping, Slide Glide, and more. Warm-ups, moves into routines, and a parent performance at end of session.

name: Little Dancers
location: Tatum Park Red Hill Activity Center
note: Basic ballet movement, terminology, and first barre introduction. Tap section builds rhythmic coordination. Ballet attire and shoes required.

name: Storytime Ballet
location: Tatum Park Red Hill Activity Center
note: Children's books + movement: listening skills, language, and coordination. Ballet attire and shoes required.

name: Tots in Tutus I
location: Tatum Park Red Hill Activity Center
note: Simple ballet exercises with professional instruction in a beautiful park setting. Creativity and enjoyment emphasized.

name: Tots in Tutus II Combo
location: Tatum Park Red Hill Activity Center
note: Ballet and tap basics using fun music and props. Builds self-confidence, listening skills, and imagination.

name: Rock to Bach - Guitar for Kids
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Group Fitness Room
dates: Mar 9–Apr 27 Mon (8 sessions); May 4–Jun 15 Mon (6 sessions, no 5/25)
note: Classical to rock: read music, strum chords, and develop technique. Small group. Required workbook; bring your own guitar.

name: Rock to Bach - Vocal for Kids
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Group Fitness Room
dates: Mar 9–Apr 27 Mon (8 sessions); May 4–Jun 15 Mon (6 sessions, no 5/25)
note: Classical harmonies to rock melodies: sheet music, hitting notes, vocal technique. Small group. Required workbook; bring your voice.

name: Baseball FUNdamentals
note: Fun, noncompetitive baseball: batting, fielding, base running, and the rules of the game.

name: T-Ball
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Gym A / Dorbrook Rec Area
dates: Mar 7–28 Sat; Apr 7–28 Tue, Apr 11–May 3, May 12–Jun 14 (multiple times)
note: Fun introduction to baseball/softball: hitting, throwing, base running, and fielding. Mini-game situations at end.

name: T-Ball 2
note: Continue developing fielding, throwing, and hitting skills. Prior T-Ball experience suggested.

name: Basketball FUNdamentals
location: Dorbrook Rec Area / Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Gym A
note: Learn shooting, dribbling, passing, and defense through age-appropriate drills and games in a fun, noncompetitive environment.

name: Basketball FUNdamentals Clinic
note: One-off basketball intro session. Fun and noncompetitive fundamentals without the full program commitment.

name: Girls' Basketball FUNdamentals
note: Basketball fundamentals for girls in a noncompetitive, non-intimidating environment with age-appropriate drills and games.

name: Girls' Basketball FUNdamentals Clinic
note: One-off girls' basketball intro. Non-intimidating, noncompetitive environment.

name: Junior Hoopsters
note: Continue developing shooting, passing, rebounding, ball handling, and defense. For athletes with beginner basketball experience.

name: Young Junior Hoopsters Clinic
note: Extra practice session for beginner-experience players wanting to keep momentum without a multi-week commitment.

name: Touché! Intro to Fencing
location: Fort Monmouth Rec Ctr Gym A
note: Swordsmanship with sportsmanship. Taught by a four-time Olympic coach. Protective gear provided. Space very limited.

name: Gymnastics - Kids
location: All American Gymnastics, Cindy Lane, Ocean
dates: Mar 7–28, Apr 11–May 2, May 9–30 Sat (multiple age groups/times)
note: Speed, strength, flexibility, and balance through gymnastics apparatus including mats and trampoline. Multiple age groups.

name: Introduction to Cheerleading
location: All American Gymnastics, Cindy Lane, Ocean
note: Basic chants, stunts, arm motions, and beginner tumbling in a positive, recreational environment.

name: Hat Trick Hockey - Floor
dates: Apr 9–30 Thu, Apr 11–May 2 Sat (10 or 11 AM), May 14–Jun 4 Thu
note: No skates or pads needed! Learn to shoot, pass, and control the puck in sneakers. Noncompetitive, builds hand-eye coordination.

name: Skyhawks Field Hockey
note: USA Field Hockey curriculum: stick handling, dribbling, passing, and receiving. All participants receive a stick and ball. For boys and girls.

name: Skyhawks Flag Football
note: USA Football curriculum: passing, receiving, kicking, and flag pulling. Confidence and life lessons in a fun, positive environment.

name: Skyhawks Lacrosse
note: Stick handling, cradling, passing, and shooting in a fun, non-checking environment. Respect, teamwork, and discipline emphasized.

name: Skyhawks Track & Field
note: Javelin, shot put, hurdles, long jump, and relay races with special equipment. Builds footspeed, flexibility, endurance, and coordination.

name: Pickleball for Kids
note: Learn rules, skills, and gameplay of one of the fastest growing sports. Youth-geared instruction. Paddles and balls provided.

name: Cool Runners Fitness Run for Fun
location: Thompson Park / Wolf Hill Rec Area
note: Build self-confidence and stamina on the trails. Running technique, form, nutrition, games, and races. Certified coach instruction.

name: Skateboarding Beginner
note: Progress and build confidence on a skateboard at Seven Presidents Oceanfront Park. Bring your own board and safety gear (loaners available).
